claims.assertion: Simulated datasets have near-machine-precision timestamp jitter (CV < 1e-4), making the jitter metric uninformative for sim data
claims.conditions: applies to datasets generated in simulation (MuJoCo, Isaac Lab, PyBullet, etc.)

evidence.notes: MuJoCo sim, scripted demos. Machine-precision jitter confirms claim for scripted sim data.
MuJoCo sim, scripted demos. Consistent with all other sim datasets. Four sim data points now supporting TEMP-001.

DROID real-hardware dataset downloaded from HuggingFace Hub. Jitter CV of 6e-06 is at sim-precision level — the LeRobot Hub version resamples timestamps to a fixed grid, making jitter uninformative for hardware characterisation. Confirms TEMP-001 from the ingestion side: Hub-served datasets always show machine-precision jitter regardless of whether the underlying data came from hardware.

falsification.condition: A simulated dataset with jitter CV > 0.01 (1%)

claims.assertion: Real hardware datasets with USB-camera + ROS recording typically show jitter CV in the range 5–25%
claims.conditions: USB camera at 20–30Hz, ROS topic recording

falsification.condition: Any real hardware dataset with jitter CV outside 1–40%

pending_tests.reason: Large-scale real hardware dataset — first test of temporal claims on hardware
Real hardware, varied setups — would characterize hardware jitter range

claims.assertion: Current dropout_rate thresholds (warning: 1%, critical: 5%) are calibrated from synthetic fixtures only and have not been validated on real hardware
claims.conditions: dropout defined as step gap > 3x median step duration

falsification.condition: A real hardware dataset where dropout_rate threshold produces false positives on known-clean data

pending_tests.reason: Known dataset — if it fires WARNING on dataset considered clean by the community, threshold needs recalibration
